seoTitle: How to Change Your Huddle Background in Slack
metaDescription: Blur your background or upload a custom image during a Slack huddle using the built-in video settings, no extra software needed.
intro: Keeping your video presence professional during quick team catch-ups doesn't require third-party virtual camera software. This guide covers blurring your background, uploading a custom image, and changing the huddle interface theme.

steps.heading: Start a huddle
steps.body: Click the headphones icon at the bottom left of a channel or direct message to begin a huddle session.

steps.heading: Turn on your camera
steps.body: Click the camera icon in the huddle bar to enable your live video feed.

steps.heading: Open video settings
steps.body: Click the three-dot menu and select 'Video Settings' to access background options.

steps.heading: Apply a blur or custom image
steps.body: Toggle the background blur, or click the plus icon to upload your own image and set it as your background.

steps.heading: Change the huddle theme
steps.body: Click 'Choose Huddle Theme' to swap the call panel's overall backdrop colors for the session.

faq.question: What image size works best for a custom background?
faq.answer: A 1920x1080 resolution image typically works best to fill the frame without stretching.

faq.question: Can I set a background blur as my permanent default?
faq.answer: Yes, you can save your preferred background setting in your profile preferences so it applies to future huddles automatically.

faq.question: Does the background blur work on mobile?
faq.answer: Background blur support on mobile can vary by device and Slack app version.

faq.question: Is changing the huddle theme the same as changing my video background?
faq.answer: No, the huddle theme changes the overall call panel's colors, while the video background only affects your camera feed.
